Facing an EEOC case against the federal government was one of the most stressful experiences of my professional life. My situation began when I was demoted from my position, and the circumstances surrounding it placed me in an extremely difficult and uncertain position. At the time, the process felt overwhelming, and I knew I needed someone who not only understood the law but also understood how challenging this journey could be emotionally.

From the very beginning, Austin T. provided exactly that. He approached my case with professionalism, knowledge, and a level of compassion that immediately put me at ease. He took the time to walk me through the EEOC process step by step, explaining what to expect and making sure I fully understood my options. His ability to simplify a complex legal process helped me feel more confident and less alone during a time when I truly needed support.

I would also like to recognize Linda, who played an important role throughout the process. Together, Austin and Linda created an environment that was both professional and supportive. They treated my concerns with respect and patience, which made a significant difference in how I experienced the entire process.

What stood out most about Austin was his ability to combine strong legal expertise with genuine care for his clients. Rather than feeling like just another case, I felt heard, respected, and supported every step of the way. What initially felt like an intimidating legal battle became a process that I could move through with confidence because I knew I had the right people advocating for me.

I am sincerely grateful for Austin’s guidance and dedication. If you are navigating an EEOC case or facing a difficult employment situation, I highly recommend Austin T. Having an attorney who is knowledgeable, trustworthy, and compassionate can make all the difference, and Austin truly embodies those qualities.    ---- Juanita

From the very beginning, working with the Devadoss Law Firm has been a professional and reassuring experience. My attorney, Madison Reed, has been an absolute Rockstar. She has a gift for keeping me calm through a very stressful process, always making sure I felt informed and supported, even when I knew her schedule was full.

What stood out most was how consistently responsive and professional the entire firm has been. Every call and message was acknowledged, and I was always kept in the loop with updates. I never felt left in the dark--instead, I felt my case was a true priority.
Madison's combination of legal skill, attention to detail, in compassion made a huge difference. She doesn't just handle the legal side --she provides real peace of mind. The law firm as a whole reflects the same professionalism, and I could always count on them to follow through.

I'm grateful to have had such strong and steady representation, and I would highly recommend Madison Reed and the Devadoss law firm to any federal employee navigating workplace challenges.

I spent my 20 year career protecting others and seeking justice for Americans. When my agency decided I was the problem I found myself in one of the hardest battles of my life, and it felt like there was no one there to help me. Then seemingly by chance, I found Devadoss and they truly stood up for me. My attorneys Madison and Octavia were kind, understanding, hard working, and put together an amazing case for me. I can't say enough good things about them and the firm.

They were knowledgeable and helpful every step of the way. When I had questions or concerns, they always spent the time I needed to ensure I was well informed. I am very grateful for the support I received and would absolutely recommend them to others.

If I had to do all over again I would go a different direction with representation. Although my case was filed with DEI. After the judge made her decision my Attorney Shane Robinson didn’t bother to call me . I was calling him. There was no empathy for me. You can appeal and there was no other communication thereafter, but I guess that’s why the attorney/firm was and still gets paid and is still being paid. And they do not mind sending payment notifications to let me know I still have a remaining balance due. I’m left with nothing but embarrassment and humiliation. Of course with everything else in the back of my mind I feel and fear retaliation. Do your research before making a life altering decision believe me it will make a difference . The Devadoss Law Firm is not it.

Called to do an intake as I’m an injured federal employee and no one answered so I left a voicemail. That call was returned by Eden, however when I called back and asked to speak with Eden regarding an intake , another young lady took my info and dismissively said ok you’ll get an email fill it out and send it back. That was a week ago, and since no return phone call and no email. Called today and Miranda was too busy to take my call , so I left the same info again, in hopes of a return call. Not sure if this is how they retain clients but I’m sure it’s not how they make new ones.
I am very satisfied with how my claim is being handled

reviews.text:
THE DEVADOSS DOES NOT DESERVE EVEN ONE STAR. I GAVE ONE STAR BECAUSE I WAS HAPPY WITH MY ASSOCIATE ATTORNEY. The Devadoss law firm charged me $19,500 in more than years. It was BETTER OFF THROWING MY HARD-WORKING MONEY OUT IN THE STREET. I filed an EEO complaint because the DISHONEST consultation attorney convinced and advised me that my case was for an EEO complaint. The Devadoss law firm took my money even though they knew I didn't have enough evidence and any merits for an EEO case. I did not realize that they don't work on privacy law. During the initial consultation, I told the attorney (who answered my phone call) that my employer violated my privacy. I also told him that, as a federal employee, I had enough evidence and merit for the Federal Privacy Act law.
However, the attorney avoided discussing Privacy law; he convinced me that my case was an EEO case. He tried to convince me that my employer discriminated against me because I was Asian and 61 years old. This attorney was not honest, and I am saying this because this attorney could tell me that his law firm does not work on Privacy law and advised me to go to another law firm that could help with my case. He just wanted clients so the Devadoss Law Firm could make money instead of helping people. In three and half years, they kept telling me I had the only option to appeal after losing every appeal. If they were honest, they could tell me they couldn't help me further after the first or second appeal. It was sad and silly that they finally told me my next option was to go on trial or appeal for reconsideration. I did not accept their advice any further because I realized that another cost for reconsideration and the reconsideration would not come in my favor. The trial will cost too much money, and I may lose. The attorney working on my case was an associate attorney who tried his best based on his ability and experience. I am very upset about this situation. Please read my review before you hire the Devadoss Law Firm.

reviews.text:
Over the phone they tell you they "work with a network of doctors" so you can get a 2nd opinion with a doctor that understands how to navigate federal worker's comp. That is a lie. They Google search until they find someone who accepts fed work comp, that is all. Their choice didn't even work out so I ended up finding my own doctor. They still billed me $900 for "provider search."

They tell you they have someone who does most of the work for less than the $300/hour lawyer rate but that is a lie. Literally every single charge was full rate, even emails and phone calls.

When they wanted another $1,000 I asked for an update and what our mutual goal was with my case; because up to that point there was no perceivable benefit. It has been 3 weeks and no one has responded. I used their online chat to get Bobby Devadoss's contact/email but they refused to provide it.

Avoid at all costs!

I have had the privilege of being represented by Michael Sheeter, Esquire, of The Devadoss Law Firm, P.L.L.C., during a challenging discrimination complaint against a federal agency. Throughout this process, Mr. Sheeter has been a steadfast advocate for my rights, consistently demonstrating exceptional legal acumen, unwavering dedication, and a genuine commitment to achieving a just outcome. His comprehensive understanding of federal employment law, coupled with his meticulous approach to case preparation, has been instrumental in navigating the complexities of this complaint. He has been readily available to address my concerns, provide clear and concise explanations of legal strategies, and offer invaluable support during a difficult time.
Mr. Sheeter's professionalism and legal expertise have been evident at every stage of this process, from the initial consultation to drafting legal documents and representing me during formal proceedings. He has skillfully gathered evidence, crafted persuasive arguments, and effectively communicated my perspective to opposing counsel and relevant authorities. His attention to detail, strategic thinking, and commitment to exploring all available legal avenues have given me confidence that my case has been handled with the utmost care and diligence. The Devadoss Law Firm itself deserves commendation for its commitment to representing federal employees.
Mr. Sheeter's exceptional work, combined with The Devadoss Law Firm's strong reputation for excellence in this specialized field, has made a lasting positive impression. Based on my personal experience and observations, I wholeheartedly recommend Michael Sheeter and The Devadoss Law Firm to any federal employee seeking legal representation in matters related to discrimination, harassment, or other employment-related concerns. Their expertise, dedication, and unwavering support can make a significant difference in protecting the rights and seeking justice for those who have experienced unfair treatment in the workplace.
